sprint: Root Studio Cache-Busting — Every <script src> Tag, Not Just the Stylesheets
task: a real, multi-message investigation that ultimately traced back to a browser-caching gap, not a logic bug. The user reported a real edit (typing new text into a World-owned "Cover titles" object via the just-shipped Selection Action Strip popup) genuinely not showing up on the visible canvas. An extensive, faithful Playwright reproduction (real mouse clicks, real keyboard typing, real canvas pixel-sampling) repeatedly showed the already-shipped repaint fix working correctly, contradicting the report. The user then confirmed reopening the same Story from My Projects showed the OLD content had come back, and decisively, a TRUE hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) fixed everything immediately. Root cause: root index.html's two stylesheet <link> tags carried a ?v= cache-busting query param since an earlier fix, but every one of its ~46 <script src> tags never adopted the same convention, unlike tools/world-builder-v2/index.html, which already stamps every script with one uniform version number per ship. A genuine, correctly-shipped JS fix could ship, verify correctly in isolation, and still fail for a real user whose browser kept serving stale pre-fix JS. Fixed: every <script src> tag in root index.html now carries the identical ?v= convention already used by its own stylesheets, with the file's own explanatory comment rewritten to document this broader, permanent rule -- bump ?v= on EVERY script AND stylesheet tag on every ship touching any JS or CSS file. Verified via the full existing Creator regression Playwright suite passing unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
